basic_details.job_title: Telangana Anganwadi Recruitment 2026 - Apply Online for 115 Anganwadi Teacher & Helper Posts in Mulugu District
basic_details.organization_name: Department of Women, Child, Disabled and Senior Citizens Welfare, Mulugu District
basic_details.short_information: This section gives a quick snapshot of the Mulugu Anganwadi Bharti 2026 for candidates who want the essential details at a glance. The recruitment covers Anganwadi Teacher and Anganwadi Helper vacancies within the two designated ICDS projects of Mulugu district.

qualification.details: Candidates asking about Mulugu Anganwadi Bharti 2026 eligibility should know that only women applicants are eligible, and qualification requirements differ slightly for AWT and AWH posts. Local residency within the concerned Anganwadi Centre area is also a mandatory condition. | For the Anganwadi Teacher (AWT) post, candidates must have passed Intermediate or 12th class as the mandatory minimum qualification. For the Anganwadi Helper (AWH) post, candidates need Intermediate qualification; however, in tribal (Girijan) areas where Intermediate-qualified candidates are not available, candidates who have passed SSC/10th class will also be considered eligible. | Candidates wondering about the Mulugu Anganwadi age limit can note that applicants must be between 18 and 35 years of age as on 1 July 2026. No separate age relaxation categories are mentioned in the notification beyond this standard age band. | Only women who are local residents of the concerned Anganwadi Centre area/Ward (in urban areas) or Gram Panchayat (in rural areas) are eligible to apply for that specific centre. For posts reserved under the ST sub-plan, only ST candidates from the same village/habitation are eligible, and similarly, SC sub-plan posts are open only to SC candidates residing in that Gram Panchayat's habitations/villages.

selection_process: Interview by District Selection Committee 10 marks
Candidates preparing for the Mulugu Anganwadi Teacher and Helper Recruitment 2026 should know that selection is based entirely on a merit-cum-interview system
with no written examination involved. Marks are awarded based on Intermediate qualification
The final merit list is prepared by the District Selection Committee
based on the combined score of Intermediate marks
and interview performance. The District Collector
who heads the District Selection Committee
holds full authority to make necessary modifications to the recruitment process or reject applications submitted through CDPO/DWO or public offices outside the official online portal.
how_to_apply: Candidates searching for the Mulugu Anganwadi Apply Online 2026 process can follow the steps below to complete their application before the 15 July 2026 deadline.
Locate the notification for Anganwadi Teacher (AWT) and Anganwadi Helper (AWH) posts, Mulugu District (No. A1/363/2026).
Read the detailed notification carefully to identify the specific Anganwadi Centre and post you are eligible for based on your residency.
Register and fill in the online application form with accurate personal, educational, and residency details.
Upload the required supporting documents as specified on the portal.
Submit the application form before 15 July 2026, 5:00 PM.
Retain a copy of the submission acknowledgment for future reference.

Applications submitted through CDPO, DWO offices, or general public grievance channels instead of the official online portal will not be considered for selection. Candidates should apply directly only through the wdcw.tg.nic.in website.

content_sections.heading: Mulugu Anganwadi Recruitment 2026 PDF Download Link
content_sections.text: Candidates who want to review the complete Mulugu Anganwadi Recruitment 2026 notification PDF, including the full list of 115 Anganwadi Centres with post-wise and category-wise vacancy details for Venkatapuram and Tadvai projects, can download it from the official website wdcw.tg.nic.in before applying online. Download Mulugu Anganwadi Recruitment 2026 PDF Download Mulugu Anganwadi Vacancy Details 2026 PDF

content_sections.heading: Mulugu Anganwadi Recruitment 2026 Overview
content_sections.text: This section gives a quick snapshot of the Mulugu Anganwadi Bharti 2026 for candidates who want the essential details at a glance. The recruitment covers Anganwadi Teacher and Anganwadi Helper vacancies within the two designated ICDS projects of Mulugu district.

content_sections.heading: Mulugu Anganwadi Recruitment 2026 Important Dates
content_sections.text: Candidates tracking the Mulugu Anganwadi Vacancy 2026 timeline should note that the entire application window spans just over two weeks. The notification was issued on 29 June 2026, with the application process beginning shortly after.

content_sections.heading: Mulugu Anganwadi Vacancy 2026 Breakdown
content_sections.text: The Mulugu AWT AWH Recruitment 2026 covers vacancies spread across 115 Anganwadi Centres (AWCs) under the Venkatapuram and Tadvai ICDS projects. Vacancies are notified for Anganwadi Teacher and/or Anganwadi Helper posts at each centre, depending on local requirement. Each Anganwadi Centre listed in the notification specifies whether the AWT post, the AWH post, or both are vacant, along with the reserved category (SC, ST, or General) applicable to that specific post. Most vacancies in the Venkatapuram and Tadvai projects are reserved for Scheduled Tribe (ST) candidates, reflecting the tribal demographic composition of these mandals, with some centres reserved for Scheduled Caste (SC) candidates.

content_sections.heading: Mulugu Anganwadi Recruitment 2026 Eligibility Criteria
content_sections.text: Candidates asking about Mulugu Anganwadi Bharti 2026 eligibility should know that only women applicants are eligible, and qualification requirements differ slightly for AWT and AWH posts. Local residency within the concerned Anganwadi Centre area is also a mandatory condition. For the Anganwadi Teacher (AWT) post, candidates must have passed Intermediate or 12th class as the mandatory minimum qualification. For the Anganwadi Helper (AWH) post, candidates need Intermediate qualification; however, in tribal (Girijan) areas where Intermediate-qualified candidates are not available, candidates who have passed SSC/10th class will also be considered eligible. Candidates wondering about the Mulugu Anganwadi age limit can note that applicants must be between 18 and 35 years of age as on 1 July 2026. No separate age relaxation categories are mentioned in the notification beyond this standard age band. Only women who are local residents of the concerned Anganwadi Centre area/Ward (in urban areas) or Gram Panchayat (in rural areas) are eligible to apply for that specific centre. For posts reserved under the ST sub-plan, only ST candidates from the same village/habitation are eligible, and similarly, SC sub-plan posts are open only to SC candidates residing in that Gram Panchayat's habitations/villages.

content_sections.heading: Mulugu Anganwadi Recruitment 2026 Selection Process
content_sections.text: Candidates preparing for the Mulugu Anganwadi Teacher and Helper Recruitment 2026 should know that selection is based entirely on a merit-cum-interview system, with no written examination involved. Marks are awarded based on Intermediate qualification, special category status, and a personal interview. The final merit list is prepared by the District Selection Committee, Mulugu, based on the combined score of Intermediate marks, applicable special category marks, and interview performance. The District Collector, who heads the District Selection Committee, holds full authority to make necessary modifications to the recruitment process or reject applications submitted through CDPO/DWO or public offices outside the official online portal.

content_sections.heading: How to Apply for Mulugu Anganwadi Recruitment 2026 Online
content_sections.text: Candidates searching for the Mulugu Anganwadi Apply Online 2026 process can follow the steps below to complete their application before the 15 July 2026 deadline. Visit the official website: https://wdcw.tg.nic.in/ Locate the notification for Anganwadi Teacher (AWT) and Anganwadi Helper (AWH) posts, Mulugu District (No. A1/363/2026). Read the detailed notification carefully to identify the specific Anganwadi Centre and post you are eligible for based on your residency. Register and fill in the online application form with accurate personal, educational, and residency details. Upload the required supporting documents as specified on the portal. Submit the application form before 15 July 2026, 5:00 PM. Retain a copy of the submission acknowledgment for future reference. Applications submitted through CDPO, DWO offices, or general public grievance channels instead of the official online portal will not be considered for selection. Candidates should apply directly only through the wdcw.tg.nic.in website.

content_sections.heading: Document Requirements for Mulugu Anganwadi Recruitment 2026
content_sections.text: Candidates applying for the Mulugu Anganwadi Vacancy 2026 must keep relevant certificates ready to support their eligibility claims during the selection process. Since selection depends heavily on category and residency proof, accurate documentation is essential. Intermediate (12th) or SSC (10th) mark sheet, as applicable to the post applied for Proof of local residency (Ward/Gram Panchayat residence proof matching the applied Anganwadi Centre) Caste certificate (SC/ST), where applying for a category-reserved post Widow certificate, for candidates claiming widow category marks Destitute status proof, for candidates claiming destitute category marks Disability certificate, for candidates claiming PwBD category marks Date of birth proof confirming age between 18 and 35 years as on 1 July 2026

content_sections.heading: Mulugu Anganwadi Recruitment 2026 Apply Online
content_sections.text: The Mulugu Anganwadi Recruitment 2026 Apply Online link is active from 1 July 2026 to 15 July 2026 (up to 5:00 PM) on the official website https://wdcw.tg.nic.in/. Candidates should apply well before the deadline to avoid last-minute submission issues.

content_sections.heading: Troubleshooting and Common Mistakes to Avoid
content_sections.text: Applicants for the Mulugu Anganwadi Teacher and Helper Recruitment 2026 often make avoidable errors that can lead to application rejection. Keeping the following points in mind can help ensure a smooth application process. Do not apply for an Anganwadi Centre outside your own Ward or Gram Panchayat, as local residency is strictly verified and non-local applications will be rejected. Ensure you apply for the correct post (AWT or AWH) matching your qualification — Intermediate for AWT, and Intermediate or SSC (in tribal areas) for AWH. Do not submit applications through CDPO, DWO offices, or public grievance portals — only applications submitted via wdcw.tg.nic.in will be considered. Double-check the category reservation (SC/ST/General) of the specific Anganwadi Centre before applying, since sub-plan posts are restricted to candidates of the same category residing in that village. Do not wait until the last date (15 July 2026, 5:00 PM) to submit your application, as last-minute technical issues could prevent timely submission. Keep all supporting documents (residency proof, caste certificate, marks memo) ready in advance, as incomplete applications may not be considered for the merit list.

faq.question: Q1. What is the last date to apply for Mulugu Anganwadi Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The last date to apply online is 15 July 2026, up to 5:00 PM.

faq.question: Q2. How many Anganwadi Centres have vacancies in Mulugu district?
faq.answer: A total of 115 Anganwadi Centres across the Venkatapuram (75 centres) and Tadvai (40 centres) ICDS projects have AWT and/or AWH vacancies notified.

faq.question: Q3. What is the eligibility for the Anganwadi Teacher (AWT) post?
faq.answer: Candidates must have passed Intermediate or 12th class and must be women aged between 18 and 35 years as on 1 July 2026.

faq.question: Q4. What is the eligibility for the Anganwadi Helper (AWH) post?
faq.answer: Candidates need Intermediate qualification; in tribal (Girijan) areas where such candidates are unavailable, SSC/10th pass candidates are also considered eligible.

faq.question: Q5. Is there an application fee for Mulugu Anganwadi Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The official notification does not specify any application fee for AWT or AWH posts.

faq.question: Q6. What is the selection process for Mulugu Anganwadi Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Selection is based on Intermediate marks (70 marks), special category marks for widow/destitute/disabled candidates (up to 20 marks), and an interview by the District Selection Committee (10 marks), totaling 100 marks.

faq.question: Q7. Can candidates apply for any Anganwadi Centre in Mulugu district?
faq.answer: No, candidates can only apply for the Anganwadi Centre within their own Ward or Gram Panchayat area, as local residency is a mandatory eligibility condition.

faq.question: Q8. Are only women eligible to apply for this recruitment?
faq.answer: Yes, both Anganwadi Teacher and Anganwadi Helper posts are reserved exclusively for women candidates as per the notification.

faq.question: Q9. What is the age limit for Mulugu Anganwadi Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Candidates must be between 18 and 35 years of age as on 1 July 2026 to be eligible for AWT or AWH posts.

faq.question: Q10. How can I apply online for Mulugu Anganwadi Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Candidates can apply online through https://wdcw.tg.nic.in/ from 1 July 2026 to 15 July 2026 by filling in the application form with accurate personal and residency details.
